textByHash.189a04fadc167316e702a6f348d03ea9c3ac1497: Hello. How can I help you?
textByHash.efa4a8acdf0f542392826e13a015f05649992f63: Oh thank Saradomin! It's about time someone responded, something needs to be done!
textByHash.6863cb7ceee155aa12d236f68880838819614f99: Of course. I was making my monthly delivery to The Sheared Ram in Lumbridge, heading east near the Draynor Village crossroads.
textByHash.5e214adae126f03bc47d707d4ce161ada875e154: Suddenly, out of the trees came a hulking monster! It chased me for a short distance, but luckily Rosie managed to spook it enough to give me a headstart.
textByHash.8b9d89924ebf9ea46993dde7a8959a25059fae06: It certainly did. I even lost my cart full of farm produce!
textByHash.0517528e221522154c8df0e00ffc3b52b622cadf: I didn't get a very good look at it I'm afraid. It was getting dark and as soon as I saw it charging at me, I ran.
textByHash.c6ba27539d4e949eaae2b53f200d592c64274849: I can still hear the sound, though. A horrible grunting... squealing...
textByHash.89f4e4aed4e35c4d35be48bae95aeaad1561e899: Is there anything else you wanted?
textByHash.0de9f0e0219100207268e911c6293cbcaf741c15: Tips...? You're supposed to be the professional monster slayer!
textByHash.364434f46e626e18e338b9386eff1ed6753d8382: Although, I suppose a good place to start would be checking the area I was attacked. See if you can find the remains of my cart.
textByHash.5e79df55fa6ca23e410e5a9d30610529a848ac7e: I remember being somewhere near the crossroads, north of Draynor Village.
textByHash.0b816b50130fd9c9bf757281d22b8350be771912: Sorry that I'm not much help. It happened so fast, the whole thing is a bit of a blur.
textByHash.9a20bded74b81403527712c008b843cede7abd4e: Oh, that'll be our lady over there by the fire. She's such a good dog, always accompanies me on deliveries.
textByHash.83358994e50ac7ae7c2c395dbefa4f59195f6c73: You can pet her if you like. She's very friendly.
textByHash.6ec901ac3538ef2028bbceb0d2e45fa8edd2cc49: Excellent! Best of luck to you, I'll be waiting right here with your reward.
textByHash.1d03c5eab90900f0735ebc97e855f81d39966a63: Oh it's you, my monster slayer! How goes the quest?
textByHash.94ea29dc4ae56fef6d6f6c68c8e4f5d34b3948c2: Sounds like you're making progress. Take care!
textByHash.7eaa27953a79154f6a761e5571e90f7c75453898: Oh that's fantastic news! I'm so relie-
textByHash.807dd4848c44ae03a6cebe29d433b87d09c03d3c: Wait, how do I know you're not just saying that to get the bounty?
textByHash.b93d2c38daded4988e1d7329ff2155f1d84e79ab: Eugh! Yes, that certainly looks about right!
textByHash.95fbba0002d99cd5dcae4faab7f54db7845cffcc: I'll probably just give it to the dog.
textByHash.b83257e99d4b659db8f4f2a3df334885f79cd5c6: I'm glad you made it back. Some strange woman in a hood came asking about the job shortly after you left. I didn't really want her back in here.
textByHash.4693ebcef91f209c08564a2a700e55aa7022fc7f: Anyway, thank you so much! Here's your reward.
textByHash.ea9b194f268741736474d8d1a44b9e0941e87cd8: Very much so! Thanks again. What was it, anyway?
textByHash.aa3fc3c1a27b0bfda71b2637c5fbea99f4bc1270: Oh crumbs... I think I've heard of those... My grandfather was always rambling on about them.
textByHash.d3e0898ec4ec6447b32f79a3407bc717f54bf409: Nevermind... that's all history now. Weren't you heading off to see Spria?
textByHash.d9b29fb47a2d31331c847113c8f315d045e83186: Of course. I'm Sarah Corvo. Not many people interested in visiting me these days. What brings you here?
textByHash.120bd1b4001ead22be55908ad2c929d4f2121041: I see. I'm afraid I can't help you. The key piece is gone.
textByHash.da0ade2beefff91cb50325894d6256bd0e125806: Yes gone. When my father passed the key piece down to me, he told me that I must keep it safe no matter what. When the vampyres came to Port Phasmatys, I buried it out in the swamps to stop them getting it.
textByHash.74744643ddd02ac35ea78027729f4604fc03fbef: You don't. The key piece is gone and it's safer that way. I never liked looking after that red lump of metal anyway.
textByHash.39adb9913c4a57b91c537370dbc261f1a8cdc130: You again. You're not still after that key piece are you?
textByHash.864f2b4bfce18b13a97c6b8c84176ebb7f25155a: Please excuse me, I'm rather busy.
textByHash.e25d9b28a4c85333299749cba53ad95fa654c7ec: I'd like a Wooden table built.
textByHash.c383cbf5cc3d1f9f9180c826ddbdd97330490d0e: I'd like a Wooden bed built.
textByHash.10679372916e820005f528d44c3905a8de5c89cd: I'd like a Wooden dresser built.
textByHash.63ede9f89855c881a4ffe96f502b3488c1fdd60b: I'd like a Wooden small table built.
textByHash.56977cd0cdd0b3c60ab48a6576f55f056d200552: I'd like a Wooden shelf built.
textByHash.eeb5b3270a87a982c52a7d711cf1db99daf6a986: I'd like a Oak table built.
textByHash.082e2dfeaa27ba8f5d3b0cde00c11f411827e24c: I'd like a Oak bed built.
textByHash.b8391a13224dc6d765b718ca310b03f55a80a924: I'd like a Oak dresser built.
textByHash.99f3d2482a476fdd402a2b42650210063565d790: I'd like a Oak small table built.
textByHash.2e9300cb67b269f65b7b08c3fb56edce568a7261: I'd like a Oak shelf built.
textByHash.a6cd885411c7de8c3f7a2cb9024e8ec9664eb6db: I'd like a Teak table built.
textByHash.66b2acfda72665d98ea4d36ee1a79742e6c1565f: I'd like a Teak bed built.
textByHash.b120eb67b88c1d7ec5fca2fb4e1120ad3d02a497: I'd like a Teak dresser built.
textByHash.0f0063a45be3f92207391bcf7d576a124fb636cc: I'd like a Teak small table built.
textByHash.92601286a6178e1218e4e68f6fd4ddcaac119ee6: I'd like a Teak shelf built.
textByHash.d5edd5f430b8b3f505758333570bec8ccab050fc: I'd like a Mahogany table built.
textByHash.a3b67d468f5869329c738ea94450a9df7b619d16: I'd like a Mahogany bed built.
textByHash.cde22774489445a76f88b1e66b93a6d1f059437f: I'd like a Mahogany dresser built.
textByHash.0d4c4089b0673941f54d0daceb07d376e75fc09d: I'd like a Mahogany small table built.
textByHash.507e8c5d1b9c69834f9e66af8492626aa4397524: I'd like a Mahogany shelf built.
textByHash.9aeac939195ac4bebb7a2b47b01f4c0b244528ef: Thank you so much! Would you like a cup of tea before you go?
textByHash.8bd8006b269d51223642cf304455907ebf9c4284: I can't find my husband, we got seperated as we disembarked the ship.
textByHash.1a1445290a6f53f6eb5f2b3f80d7831cc3a62178: You're the one who stopped Necrovarus. Our people are forever grateful to you, even if we don't always show it.
textByHash.ab67f34ddac83a8de6a8c8cc051e034f5e6f0503: Well, it's actually my loom, but I don't mind you using it, if you like. You can use it to weave sacks and baskets in which you can put vegetables and fruit.
textByHash.b1944b42a1281d832bed597a59c558c1707ecbbe: Well, the best sacks are made with jute fibres; you can grow jute yourself in a hops patch. I'd say about 4 jute fibres should be enough to weave a sack.
textByHash.2b8db7c14eb7bb921faf18743d8acf7defe2e198: Well, the best baskets are made with young branches cut from a willow tree. You'll need a very young willow tree; otherwise, the branches will have grown too thick to be able to weave. I suggest growing your own.
textByHash.e95caef4fa829c4747c056a7fb1912ba5842416e: You can cut the branches with a standard pair of secateurs. You will probably need about 6 willow branches to weave a complete basket.
